rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE EDWARD LITKE REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Granting Reformation Of Trust
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 07/12/2023. Notice of the amended petition must be given with a copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(D)(6). A courtesy copy of the amended petition must also be provided. The following issues remain unaddressed (or must be additionally addressed): 1) No proposed order has been received. 2) 26 CFR 26.2654-1(b)(1)(ii)(B) requires a reformation proceeding, if required, to be commenced prior to the filing deadline for the estate tax return, but this petition does not appear to have been filed prior to the deadline. Has the IRS granted an extension for the judicial reformation sought by the petition? 3) If the drafting attorneys were not licensed to practice law in California, did their preparation of the subject trust governed by California law for the settlor as a California resident client constitute the unauthorized practice of law in California? 4) How did the status of the settlor's health, his location in San Francisco, and his concerns about the imminent death cause the omission of the requirement intended by the settlor to divide the Residual Trust into GST tax exempt and nonexempt trusts in the event the settlor was to predecease his spouse? Is either drafting attorney admitting that he caused the error?

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF SAUL TRUJILLO
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order To Pay Attorney Fees Under Probate Code
rulings.Rulings: Matter will be continued out at the hearing for the procedural issues to be cleared: 1. Date of value on I&A must be the date of appointment. 2. I&A filed on 5/3/23 shows the conservatorship of estate value as dated of the conservatee's death. I&A shall specify appropriate date of value and characterization of the assets. Also, neither conservator nor attorney dated. 3. I&A and allegation on Accounting petition must mirror each other. I&A shows over 1 million dollar value while petition alleges that the value was $69,626.74. 4. I&A lists $1,038,177 in total. Need clarification if this amount represents the conservatee's interests only. 5. Notice of Hearing. Caroline was not noticed. Also, Notice of Hearing form DE-120 must be used. A copy of petition must be served with the notice. 6. Fee petition to be supported by declaration describing services in detail. Mere statement of "reasonable compensation" is not sufficient. 7. Need under which authority the conservator can transfer all assets to the surviving spouse. Also, Petition to list all assets subject to distribution in detail. PC 2623(a)(5) 8. Need verification of cash balances pursuant to LRSF 14.34.D. 9. Billing statement for the facility. 10. Clarification why not CAA fees should be paid by the Conservatorship of Estate.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF SAUL TRUJILLO
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report
rulings.Rulings: Hearing to continue the matter. The procedural issues to be cleared: 1. Date of value on I&A must be the date of appointment. 2. I&A filed on 5/3/23 shows the conservatorship of estate value as dated of the conservatee's death. I&A shall specify appropriate date of value and characterization of the assets. Also, neither conservator nor attorney dated. 3. I&A and allegation on Accounting petition must mirror each other. I&A shows over 1 million dollar value while petition alleges that the value was $69,626.74. 4. I&A lists $1,038,177 in total. Need clarification if this amount represents the conservatee's interests only. 5. Notice of Hearing. Caroline (daughter) and Court Appointed Counsel not noticed. Also, Notice of Hearing form DE-120 must be used. A copy of petition must be served with the notice. 6. Fee petition to be supported by declaration describing services in detail. Mere statement of "reasonable compensation" is not sufficient. 7. Need under which authority the conservator can transfer all assets to the surviving spouse. Also, Petition to list all assets subject to distribution in detail. PC 2623(a)(5) 8. Need verification of cash balances pursuant to LRSF 14.34.D. 9. Billing statement for the facility. 10. Clarification why not CAA fees should be paid by the Conservatorship of Estate.
